events.description: Fired when a user sends a message to an AI agent. Captures session/trace/turn context, message content (via $llm_message chunking), implicit feedback signals (regeneration, edit), and file attachments. The entry point for user-initiated AI interactions.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Agent ID
properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Message ID
properties.description: Unique identifier for this message event (UUID). Used to link scores and tool calls back to specific messages.

properties.name: [Agent] Component Type
properties.description: Type of component that produced this event: 'user_input', 'llm', 'tool', 'embedding'.

properties.name: [Agent] Locale
properties.description: User locale (e.g., 'en-US').

properties.name: [Amplitude] Session Replay ID
properties.description: Links to Amplitude Session Replay (format: device_id/session_id). Enables one-click navigation from AI session to browser replay.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Regeneration
properties.description: Whether the user requested the AI regenerate a previous response.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Edit
properties.description: Whether the user edited a previous message and resubmitted.

properties.name: [Agent] Edited Message ID
properties.description: The message_id of the original message that was edited (links the edit to the original).

properties.name: [Agent] Has Attachments
properties.description: Whether this message includes file attachments (uploads, images, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Attachment Types
properties.description: Distinct attachment types (e.g., 'pdf', 'image', 'csv'). Serialized JSON array.

properties.name: [Agent] Attachment Count
properties.description: Number of file attachments included with this message.

properties.name: [Agent] Total Attachment Size Bytes
properties.description: Total size of all attachments in bytes.

properties.name: [Agent] Attachments
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of attachment metadata (type, name, size_bytes, mime_type). Only metadata, never file content.

properties.name: [Agent] Message Labels
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of MessageLabel objects (key-value pairs with optional confidence). Used for routing tags, classifier output, business context.

properties.name: [Agent] Message Source
properties.description: Origin of the message: 'user' for direct user input, 'agent' for messages delegated from a parent agent (auto-set based on parentAgentId).

events.description: Fired when an AI agent generates a response. Captures model, provider, latency, TTFB, token breakdowns (input/output/reasoning/cache), cache-aware cost, finish reason, reasoning content, system prompt, model configuration, copy signal, and model tier. The primary event for LLM performance and cost analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Agent ID
properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Message ID
properties.description: Unique identifier for this message event (UUID). Used to link scores and tool calls back to specific messages.

properties.name: [Agent] Component Type
properties.description: Type of component that produced this event: 'user_input', 'llm', 'tool', 'embedding'.

properties.name: [Agent] Model Name
properties.description: LLM model identifier (e.g., 'gpt-4o', 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514').

properties.name: [Agent] Provider
properties.description: LLM provider name (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'google', 'mistral', 'bedrock').

properties.name: [Agent] Latency Ms
properties.description: Total wall-clock latency in milliseconds for this operation.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Error
properties.description: Whether this event represents an error condition.

properties.name: [Agent] Error Message
properties.description: Error message text when Is Error is true.

properties.name: [Agent] Locale
properties.description: User locale (e.g., 'en-US').

properties.name: [Agent] Span Kind
properties.description: Classification of the span type for OTEL bridge compatibility.

properties.name: [Amplitude] Session Replay ID
properties.description: Links to Amplitude Session Replay (format: device_id/session_id). Enables one-click navigation from AI session to browser replay.

properties.name: [Agent] TTFB Ms
properties.description: Time to first byte/token in milliseconds. Measures perceived responsiveness for streaming.

properties.name: [Agent] Input Tokens
properties.description: Number of input/prompt tokens consumed by this LLM call.

properties.name: [Agent] Output Tokens
properties.description: Number of output/completion tokens generated by this LLM call.

properties.name: [Agent] Total Tokens
properties.description: Total tokens consumed (input + output).

properties.name: [Agent] Reasoning Tokens
properties.description: Tokens consumed by reasoning/thinking (o1, o3, extended thinking models).

properties.name: [Agent] Cache Read Tokens
properties.description: Input tokens served from the provider's prompt cache (cheaper rate). Used for cache-aware cost calculation.

properties.name: [Agent] Cache Creation Tokens
properties.description: Input tokens that created new prompt cache entries.

properties.name: [Agent] Cost USD
properties.description: Estimated cost in USD for this LLM call. Cache-aware when cache token counts are provided.

properties.name: [Agent] Finish Reason
properties.description: Why the model stopped generating: 'stop', 'end_turn', 'tool_use', 'length', 'content_filter', etc.

properties.name: [Agent] Tool Calls
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of tool call requests made by the AI in this response.

properties.name: [Agent] Has Reasoning
properties.description: Whether the AI response included reasoning/thinking content.

properties.name: [Agent] Reasoning Content
properties.description: The AI's reasoning/thinking content (when available and content_mode permits).

properties.name: [Agent] System Prompt
properties.description: The system prompt used for this LLM call (when content_mode permits). Chunked for long prompts.

properties.name: [Agent] System Prompt Length
properties.description: Character length of the system prompt.

properties.name: [Agent] Temperature
properties.description: Temperature parameter used for this LLM call.

properties.name: [Agent] Max Output Tokens
properties.description: Maximum output tokens configured for this LLM call.

properties.name: [Agent] Top P
properties.description: Top-p (nucleus sampling) parameter used for this LLM call.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Streaming
properties.description: Whether this response was generated via streaming.

properties.name: [Agent] Prompt ID
properties.description: Identifier for the prompt template or version used.

properties.name: [Agent] Was Copied
properties.description: Whether the user copied this AI response content. An implicit positive quality signal.

properties.name: [Agent] Was Cached
properties.description: Whether this response was served from a semantic/full-response cache (distinct from token-level prompt caching).

properties.name: [Agent] Model Tier
properties.description: Model tier classification: 'fast' (GPT-4o-mini, Haiku, Flash), 'standard' (GPT-4o, Sonnet, Pro), or 'reasoning' (o1, o3, DeepSeek-R1). Auto-inferred from model name.

properties.name: [Agent] Has Attachments
properties.description: Whether this AI response includes generated attachments (images, charts, files).

properties.name: [Agent] Attachment Types
properties.description: Distinct attachment types in this AI response. Serialized JSON array.

properties.name: [Agent] Attachment Count
properties.description: Number of attachments generated by the AI in this response.

properties.name: [Agent] Total Attachment Size Bytes
properties.description: Total size of all AI-generated attachments in bytes.

properties.name: [Agent] Attachments
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of AI-generated attachment metadata.

properties.name: [Agent] Message Labels
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of MessageLabel objects attached to this AI response.

properties.name: [Agent] Message Label Map
properties.description: Serialized JSON map of label key to value for quick lookup.

events.description: Fired when an AI agent invokes a tool or function. Captures tool name, input/output (serialized JSON, respects content_mode), latency, success status, and error details. Use to identify which tools are most frequently used, slowest, and most error-prone.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Agent ID
properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Component Type
properties.description: Type of component that produced this event: 'user_input', 'llm', 'tool', 'embedding'.

properties.name: [Agent] Latency Ms
properties.description: Total wall-clock latency in milliseconds for this operation.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Error
properties.description: Whether this event represents an error condition.

properties.name: [Agent] Error Message
properties.description: Error message text when Is Error is true.

properties.name: [Agent] Locale
properties.description: User locale (e.g., 'en-US').

properties.name: [Agent] Span Kind
properties.description: Classification of the span type for OTEL bridge compatibility.

properties.name: [Amplitude] Session Replay ID
properties.description: Links to Amplitude Session Replay (format: device_id/session_id). Enables one-click navigation from AI session to browser replay.

properties.name: [Agent] Invocation ID
properties.description: Unique identifier for this tool invocation (UUID). Used to link tool calls to parent messages.

properties.name: [Agent] Tool Name
properties.description: Name of the tool/function that was invoked (e.g., 'search_docs', 'web_search').

properties.name: [Agent] Tool Success
properties.description: Whether the tool call completed successfully.

properties.name: [Agent] Tool Input
properties.description: Serialized JSON of the tool's input arguments. Only sent when content_mode='full'.

properties.name: [Agent] Tool Output
properties.description: Serialized JSON of the tool's output/return value. Only sent when content_mode='full'.

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Message ID
properties.description: The message_id of the user message that triggered this tool call. Links the tool call into the event graph.

events.description: Fired when a vector embedding operation is performed. Captures model, provider, latency, input tokens, vector dimensions, and cost. Used for RAG pipeline analytics.

properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Provider
properties.description: LLM provider name (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'google', 'mistral', 'bedrock').

properties.name: [Agent] Latency Ms

properties.name: [Agent] Span ID

properties.name: [Agent] Input Tokens

properties.name: [Agent] Embedding Dimensions
properties.description: Dimensionality of the output embedding vector.

properties.name: [Agent] Cost USD
properties.description: Estimated cost in USD for this embedding operation.

events.description: Fired for a generic pipeline operation such as vector search, rerank, guardrails, or any custom step. Captures span name, input/output state (serialized JSON), latency, and parent span hierarchy. Enables pipeline step analysis and latency attribution.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Latency Ms
properties.description: Total wall-clock latency in milliseconds for this operation.

properties.name: [Agent] Is Error
properties.description: Whether this event represents an error condition.

properties.name: [Agent] Error Message
properties.description: Error message text when Is Error is true.

properties.name: [Agent] Span ID
properties.description: Unique identifier for this span (UUID).

properties.name: [Agent] Span Name
properties.description: Name of the operation (e.g., 'rag_pipeline', 'vector_search', 'rerank').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Span ID
properties.description: Span ID of the parent span for nested pipeline steps.

properties.name: [Agent] Input State
properties.description: Serialized JSON of the span's input state. Only sent when content_mode='full'.

properties.name: [Agent] Output State
properties.description: Serialized JSON of the span's output state. Only sent when content_mode='full'.

events.description: Fired when a session is explicitly closed by the SDK (via track_session_end() or Session context manager exit). Can carry optional enrichments and abandonment tracking. Triggers server-side enrichment when content_mode='full'.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Agent ID
properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Enrichments
properties.description: Serialized JSON of SessionEnrichments (topic classifications, rubric scores, outcome, flags). Attached when enrichments are provided at session close.

properties.name: [Agent] Abandonment Turn
properties.description: Turn ID of the last user message that received an AI response before the user left. Low values (e.g., 1) strongly signal first-response dissatisfaction.

properties.name: [Agent] Session Idle Timeout Minutes
properties.description: Custom idle timeout for this session (default 30 min). Tells the server how long to wait before auto-closing.

events.description: Fired when a customer provides structured session classifications via track_session_enrichment(). Used with content_mode='customer_enriched' for teams who run their own classifiers. Distinct from the server-generated [Agent] Session Evaluation. Contains serialized SessionEnrichments (topics, rubrics, outcome, flags) as a single JSON property.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Enrichments
properties.description: Serialized JSON of SessionEnrichments: topic_classifications, rubrics, overall_outcome, quality_score, sentiment_score, boolean flags, agent chain metadata, and message labels.

events.description: Quality signal attached to a message or session. Covers user feedback (source='user', e.g., thumbs up/down), automated evals (source='ai', e.g., LLM-as-judge), human annotations (source='reviewer'), and server-generated rubric scores. All quality signals share this event type, enabling unified queries across signal sources.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Trace ID
properties.description: Identifies one user-message-to-AI-response cycle within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn ID
properties.description: Monotonically increasing counter for event ordering within a session.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Parent Agent ID
properties.description: For multi-agent orchestration: the agent that delegated to this agent.

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

properties.name: [Agent] Context
properties.description: Serialized JSON dict of arbitrary segmentation dimensions (experiment_variant, surface, feature_flag, prompt_revision, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Env
properties.description: Deployment environment: 'production', 'staging', or 'dev'.

properties.name: [Agent] SDK Version
properties.description: Version of the amplitude-ai SDK that produced this event.

properties.name: [Agent] Runtime
properties.description: SDK runtime: 'python' or 'node'.

properties.name: [Agent] Score Name
properties.description: Name of the score (e.g., 'user-feedback', 'task_completion', 'accuracy', 'groundedness').

properties.name: [Agent] Score Value
properties.description: Numeric score value. Binary (0/1), continuous (0.0-1.0), or rating scale (1-5).

properties.name: [Agent] Target ID
properties.description: The message_id or session_id being scored.

properties.name: [Agent] Target Type
properties.description: What is being scored: 'message' or 'session'.

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluation Source
properties.description: Source of the evaluation: 'user' (end-user feedback), 'ai' (automated/server pipeline), or 'reviewer' (human expert).

properties.name: [Agent] Comment
properties.description: Optional text explanation for the score (respects content_mode).

properties.name: [Agent] Taxonomy Version
properties.description: Which taxonomy config version produced this enrichment (from ai_category_config.config_version_id).

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluated At
properties.description: Epoch milliseconds when this enrichment/evaluation was computed.

properties.name: [Agent] Score Label
properties.description: Direction-neutral magnitude label derived from score value. Default 5-tier: very_high (>=0.8), high (>=0.6), moderate (>=0.4), low (>=0.2), very_low (>=0.0). Server-side only.

events.description: Fired once per enriched session by the server-side enrichment pipeline. Contains session-level facts: overall outcome, turn count, boolean flags (has_task_failure, has_negative_feedback, has_technical_failure, has_data_quality_issues), cost, and behavioral patterns. Enables cohorts like 'users with 3+ task failures in 30 days'.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluation Source
properties.description: Source of the evaluation: 'user' (end-user feedback), 'ai' (automated/server pipeline), or 'reviewer' (human expert).

properties.name: [Agent] Taxonomy Version
properties.description: Which taxonomy config version produced this enrichment (from ai_category_config.config_version_id).

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluated At
properties.description: Epoch milliseconds when this enrichment/evaluation was computed.

properties.name: [Agent] Overall Outcome
properties.description: Session outcome classification: 'success', 'partial_success', 'failure', 'abandoned', 'response_provided', etc.

properties.name: [Agent] Turn Count
properties.description: Number of conversation turns in this session.

properties.name: [Agent] Session Total Tokens
properties.description: Total LLM tokens consumed across all turns in this session.

properties.name: [Agent] Session Avg Latency Ms
properties.description: Average AI response latency in milliseconds across the session.

properties.name: [Agent] Request Complexity
properties.description: Complexity classification of the user's request: 'simple', 'moderate', 'complex', or 'ambiguous'.

properties.name: [Agent] Has Task Failure
properties.description: Whether the agent failed to complete the user's request.

properties.name: [Agent] Has Negative Feedback
properties.description: Whether the user expressed dissatisfaction during the session.

properties.name: [Agent] Has Technical Failure
properties.description: Whether technical errors occurred (tool timeouts, API failures, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Has Data Quality Issues
properties.description: Whether the AI output had data quality problems (wrong data, hallucinations, etc.).

properties.name: [Agent] Models Used
properties.description: LLM models used in this session. JSON array of strings.

properties.name: [Agent] Root Agent Name
properties.description: Entry-point agent in multi-agent flows.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Chain Depth
properties.description: Number of agents in the delegation chain.

properties.name: [Agent] Task Failure Type
properties.description: Specific failure type when has_task_failure is true (e.g., 'wrong_answer', 'unable_to_complete').

properties.name: [Agent] Technical Error Count
properties.description: Count of technical errors that occurred during the session.

properties.name: [Agent] Error Categories
properties.description: Categorized error types (e.g., 'chart_not_found', 'timeout'). JSON array of strings.

properties.name: [Agent] Behavioral Patterns
properties.description: Detected behavioral anti-patterns (e.g., 'retry_storm', 'clarification_loop', 'early_abandonment'). JSON array of strings.

properties.name: [Agent] Session Cost USD
properties.description: Total LLM cost in USD for this AI session (aggregated from per-message costs).

properties.name: [Agent] Enrichment Cost USD
properties.description: Cost in USD of running the enrichment pipeline's LLM inference for this session. Distinct from the session's own LLM cost.

properties.name: [Agent] Quality Score
properties.description: Overall quality score (0.0-1.0) computed by the enrichment pipeline for this session.

properties.name: [Agent] Sentiment Score
properties.description: User sentiment score (0.0-1.0) inferred from the conversation by the enrichment pipeline.

properties.name: [Agent] Task Failure Reason
properties.description: Explanation of why the task failed when has_task_failure is true (e.g., 'chart data source unavailable').

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Chain
properties.description: Serialized JSON array of agent IDs representing the delegation chain in multi-agent flows.

properties.name: [Agent] Project ID
properties.description: Amplitude project ID that owns the AI session being evaluated.

properties.name: [Agent] Has User Feedback
properties.description: Whether the session received explicit user feedback (thumbs up/down, rating).

properties.name: [Agent] User Score
properties.description: Aggregate user feedback score for the session (0.0-1.0). Present only when has_user_feedback is true.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Version
properties.description: Agent code version (e.g., 'v4.2'). Enables version-over-version quality comparison.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent Description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose (e.g., 'Handles user chat requests via OpenAI GPT-4o'). Enables observability-driven agent registry from event streams.

events.description: Fired once per topic model per session by the server-side enrichment pipeline. A session with 3 topic models (e.g., query_intent, product_area, error_domain) produces 3 of these events. Property names are always the same; the topic model name goes in the value. Enables funnels like 'AI session about charts -> Chart Created'.

properties.name: [Agent] Session ID
properties.description: Unique session identifier. All events in one conversation share the same session ID.

properties.name: [Agent] Agent ID
properties.description: Identifies which AI agent handled the interaction (e.g., 'support-bot', 'houston').

properties.name: [Agent] Customer Org ID
properties.description: Organization ID for multi-tenant platforms. Enables account-level group analytics.

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluation Source
properties.description: Source of the evaluation: 'user' (end-user feedback), 'ai' (automated/server pipeline), or 'reviewer' (human expert).

properties.name: [Agent] Taxonomy Version
properties.description: Which taxonomy config version produced this enrichment (from ai_category_config.config_version_id).

properties.name: [Agent] Evaluated At
properties.description: Epoch milliseconds when this enrichment/evaluation was computed.

properties.name: [Agent] Topic
properties.description: Which topic model this classification is for (e.g., 'product_area', 'query_intent', 'error_domain').

properties.name: [Agent] Selection Mode
properties.description: Whether this topic model uses 'single' (MECE) or 'multiple' (multi-label) selection.

properties.name: [Agent] Primary
properties.description: Primary classification value (e.g., 'charts', 'billing_issues').

properties.name: [Agent] Secondary
properties.description: Secondary classifications for multi-label topics. JSON array of strings.

properties.name: [Agent] Subcategories
properties.description: Subcategories for finer classification within the primary topic (e.g., 'TREND_ANALYSIS', 'WRONG_EVENT'). JSON array of strings.
